metadata.title: Jisr · Your online English classroom, in a single tool
metadata.description: Jisr brings your lessons, quizzes, and student tracking together. AI handles the repetitive work — you keep the teaching and the relationship with your students.

hero.subtitle: Jisr brings your lessons, quizzes, and student tracking together. AI handles the repetitive work. You keep what matters: the teaching and the relationship with your students.
hero.ctaNote: Invitation only · we chat for 15 min before opening access

pain.titleTail: The rest, you do because you have to.
pain.lede: You plan, you teach, you support. That's your job. The rest is 5 open tabs, never-ending messages, and papers to grade at 11 PM. That shouldn't be your problem.
pain.note1Sub: You drop everything to reply. Again.
pain.note2Sub: And the week hasn't even started.
pain.prepSub: PowerPoint, Word, Google Forms. One tab per tool.

conviction.p1: We've watched teachers lose their hours to the repetitive. They're here to create, invent, adapt to each student. Not to redo the same corrections every evening.
conviction.p2Lead: We figured it shouldn't work that way. That digital tools and AI, if they're good for anything, are good for
conviction.p3: So we built Jisr. Your lessons, your quizzes, your sessions, your students. In a single tool. With AI that takes what repeats: corrections, prep, tracking.
conviction.provenance: Built in Morocco, with a teacher running online classes with her own students: high school, IELTS, TOEFL. Nothing ships until it's been used with real students.
conviction.imageAlt: A teacher standing before a bridge stretching toward warm light — an open hand waits at the other end, surrounded by a constellation of tools.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: From the first message to your first class — three steps.
howItWorks.step1Description: On WhatsApp or by phone, we get your context: number of students, levels, what takes up the most time today.
howItWorks.step2Description: If Jisr is a fit, you receive your personal access link within 48 hours.
howItWorks.step3Description: Add your students, build your first course, schedule your sessions — from your dashboard, with no middleman.

features.lede: We handle the repetitive tasks. We watch over your students. You build your classes your way.
features.f1Sub: Build lessons, materials, paths by CEFR level. Share with your students in one click.
features.f2Sub: Lessons, quizzes, audio passages with questions. Describe the theme, AI proposes. You stay in control.
features.f3Sub: Multiple choice grades itself. Writing: AI does the first pass, you approve in 2 minutes.
features.f4Sub: Engagement, scores, who's drifting. You see it before the student leaves.
features.f5Sub: Messages, homework, resources, live sessions. Your WhatsApp can breathe.
features.footerLead: Jisr is the bridge between you and your students. Not yet another platform to learn.

speakingGap.title: Moroccans read English well. They just can't hear it yet.
speakingGap.lede: A 72-point gap between reading and listening comprehension — documented by the EF English Proficiency Index 2025. That's the opportunity for every English teacher in Morocco.
speakingGap.kicker: Jisr is built around that gap. Listening quizzes with AI-generated audio, oral corrections, communication exercises — everything pushes toward speaking.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you want to know before getting started.
faq.q1Question: Why can't I create an account directly?
faq.q1Answer: Jisr is in early access — we open accounts gradually so we can properly onboard each teacher. We take 15 minutes (WhatsApp or phone, your choice) to understand your context, then send you an invitation if Jisr is a fit. No fluff, no hard sell.
faq.q2Question: How does it work with WhatsApp?
faq.q2Answer: Jisr complements WhatsApp, it doesn't replace it. Keep WhatsApp for quick communication, use Jisr for lessons, quizzes, and tracking.
faq.q3Question: Do my current students have to sign up themselves?
faq.q3Answer: No. You add your students manually from your dashboard. They get access without having to find your profile.

stakes.item1: 47 unread WhatsApp messages on Tuesday night.
stakes.item3: 5 hours of prep, every week. Always.
stakes.item4: And still no time for the students knocking at your door.

finalCta.title: Try Jisr ahead of launch.
finalCta.subtitle: Leave your details — we'll reach out at launch.
finalCta.bullet2: Your feedback directly shapes the product
finalCta.bullet3: No commitment — test it at your own pace
finalCta.labelTimeSink: What takes the most time outside of class
finalCta.footnote1: Anything specific to mention? You can tell us during the call.
finalCta.footnote2: No spam. One message at launch.
finalCta.errorTimeSink: Select what takes the most time
finalCta.alreadyOnList: You're already on the list — we'll keep you posted.
finalCta.submitError: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.
finalCta.success: Got it — we'll contact you at launch.
finalCta.situationSchool: Teacher at a school or language center

footer.tagline: The bridge between you and your students. No middleman.

layout.panelSubtitle: Your courses, your students, your quizzes, your tracking — all in one place.
layout.bullet1: Quizzes generated in seconds — audio included.
layout.bullet2: Automatic grading. You approve in 2 minutes.
layout.bullet3: Alerts when a student starts to drift.
layout.studentNote: Student? Sign in — your instructor has registered you.

login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue your learning
login.oauthNoAccount: No account is linked to that Google address. Ask your instructor for an invitation or contact us.
login.oauthFailed: Google sign-in failed. Please try again.

register.title: Access by invitation
register.subtitle: Account creation is by invitation only.
register.studentsBody: Your instructor will send you an invitation link by email to join their courses.
register.instructorsBody: Contact us to request access to the platform.

instructorSignup.welcomeSubtitle: Create your instructor account to get started.
instructorSignup.invalidInvite: This invitation is invalid, expired or already used. Please contact the person who sent it to you.
instructorSignup.wrongInviteKind: This link is not an instructor invitation.
instructorSignup.emailLockedNote: This invitation is tied to this address.
instructorSignup.errorAutoLogin: Account created, but automatic sign-in failed.
instructorSignup.errorEmailMismatch: The Google address you used does not match the address of your invitation.
instructorSignup.errorInviteInvalid: This invitation is invalid or has already been used.
instructorSignup.errorConsumeFailed: An error occurred while finalizing your account. Please try again.

common.translationDisclaimer: This English version is provided for the convenience of English-speaking users. The French version is the original. In case of any discrepancy between the two versions, the French version prevails.

terms.metaDescription: The terms governing use of the Jisr platform by teachers and students.
terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.objetBody: These terms govern access to and use of Jisr, a teaching-assistance platform for English teachers and their students. By creating an account or using the service, you accept these terms.
terms.editeurBody: Jisr is operated by Ayoub [Full name — TO BE COMPLETED], acting as an individual in Morocco. Contact: {email}.
terms.comptesItem1: Teachers: create courses, lessons, quizzes, and manage their students.
terms.comptesItem2: Students: access the courses to which their teacher has enrolled them; they do not sign up themselves.
terms.comptesItem3: You are responsible for keeping your credentials confidential and for any activity carried out from your account.
terms.comptesBody2: A teacher who creates accounts for their students undertakes to have the necessary authorizations, including parental consent for minors.
terms.usageItem1: Use the service for unlawful or harmful purposes.
terms.usageItem2: Attempt to access other users' data without authorization.
terms.usageItem3: Interfere with the operation of the platform (overload, bypass of limits, reverse engineering).
terms.usageItem4: Upload unlawful or defamatory content, or content infringing third-party rights.
terms.contenuBody: You retain ownership of the content you create or upload (courses, lessons, documents). You grant us a limited license to store and display that content for the sole purpose of providing the service. The Jisr code, design and brand remain our property.
terms.iaBody: Jisr offers content-generation and grading tools assisted by artificial intelligence. These outputs are aids and may contain errors; the teacher remains responsible for verifying the content before using it with their students. AI use may be subject to limits depending on your plan.
terms.paiementBody: The service offers a free plan and paid plans. The pricing terms in effect are communicated at the time of subscription. During the launch phase, access may be provided free of charge or at a preferential rate to founding users.
terms.responsabiliteBody: We endeavor to provide a reliable service, but the service is provided "as is", without warranty of uninterrupted availability. To the extent permitted by law, we shall not be liable for any indirect damages or loss of data resulting from non-compliant use.
terms.resiliationBody: You may stop using the service at any time. We may suspend or terminate an account in case of breach of these terms, with prior notice where possible.
terms.droitBody: These terms are governed by Moroccan law. Any dispute shall fall under the jurisdiction of the competent courts of Rabat, Morocco.

privacy.metaDescription: How Jisr collects, uses and protects personal data, in accordance with Moroccan Law 09-08.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.responsableHeading: Who is responsible for your data
privacy.responsableP1: Jisr is a teaching-assistance platform for English teachers in Morocco. The data controller is Ayoub [Full name — TO BE COMPLETED], founder of Jisr, reachable at {email}.
privacy.responsableP2: This processing is governed by Moroccan Law No. 09-08 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data, under the supervision of the National Commission for the Control of the Protection of Personal Data (CNDP).
privacy.responsableP3: CNDP filing number: [TO BE COMPLETED after filing].
privacy.donneesIntro: Depending on whether you are a teacher or a student, we collect:
privacy.donneesItem1: Account data: name, email address, role (teacher, student, administrator), CEFR level for students.
privacy.donneesItem2: Teaching content: courses, lessons, quizzes, documents that you create or upload.
privacy.donneesItem3: Student activity data: quiz attempts, answers, completed lessons, attendance at sessions, last activity dates.
privacy.donneesItem4: Minimal technical data: error logs (via Sentry) and essential session cookies.
privacy.donneesBody2: We do not collect any sensitive data within the meaning of Law 09-08 (health, political or religious opinions, etc.).
privacy.usageItem1: Providing the service: creating accounts, displaying courses, enabling quiz attempts and student tracking.
privacy.usageItem2: Generating AI-assisted teaching content (quizzes, lessons, grading) at the teacher's request.
privacy.usageItem3: Allowing teachers to track the progress of their own students.
privacy.usageItem4: Ensuring security, detecting errors and improving the product.
privacy.usageBody2: Aggregated learning data is never sold or exploited as a source of revenue; it is used only internally to improve the product.
privacy.mineursBody: Some students may be minors. Student accounts are created and managed by the teacher, who is responsible for obtaining the necessary parental consent before enrolling a minor student. We limit collection to what is strictly necessary for teaching follow-up.
privacy.accesHeading: Who has access to your data
privacy.accesItem1: Your teacher (for students): they only see the activity and results of their own students.
privacy.accesItem2: Yourself: you access your own data.
privacy.accesItem3: The Jisr team: limited access for support and maintenance.
privacy.accesBody2: Students never see the personally identifying data of other students.
privacy.transfertP1: In order to operate, Jisr relies on providers whose servers are located outside Morocco. This constitutes an international transfer of data within the meaning of Law 09-08:
privacy.transfertItem1: Supabase — database, authentication and file storage.
privacy.transfertItem3: Anthropic and OpenAI — AI content generation and grading.
privacy.transfertP2: These transfers are subject to the required formalities with the CNDP [transfer authorization — TO BE COMPLETED / in progress]. We choose providers offering recognized security guarantees.
privacy.conservationBody: We keep your data for as long as your account is active. When a teacher removes a student from a course, history is retained by default to allow for possible re-enrollment; a permanent deletion can be requested. When an account is closed, data is deleted or anonymized within a reasonable period, except where retention is required by law.
privacy.droitsIntro: In accordance with Law 09-08, you have the following rights:
privacy.droitsItem1: Right of access to your data.
privacy.droitsItem2: Right to rectification of inaccurate data.
privacy.droitsItem3: Right to object on legitimate grounds.
privacy.droitsItem4: Right to erasure of your data.
privacy.droitsBody2: To exercise these rights, write to us at {email}. You may also refer the matter to the CNDP (www.cndp.ma) in case of a dispute.
privacy.securiteBody: We apply technical and organizational measures: encryption of communications (HTTPS), access control at the database level (Row Level Security), separation of roles, and error monitoring. As no system is infallible, we undertake to inform you in the event of an incident affecting your data.
privacy.modificationsBody: We may update this policy. The date of the last update appears in the table of contents. In the event of a material change, we will inform you.

cookies.metaDescription: Cookies used by Jisr: only cookies essential to the operation of the service.
cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.utilisationBody: Jisr only uses cookies that are strictly necessary for the operation of the service. We do not use any advertising cookies, nor any tracking or behavioral analytics tools.
cookies.essentielsItem1: Session cookie (authentication): keeps you securely signed in. Managed by our authentication provider (Supabase).
cookies.essentielsItem2: Interface preference: remembers the display state of the sidebar for your convenience.
cookies.essentielsBody2: Because these cookies are indispensable to the operation of the platform, they do not require prior consent. Without them, you would not be able to sign in or use the service.
cookies.gererBody: You can configure your browser to block or delete cookies. Note however that blocking essential cookies will prevent you from signing in to your account.
cookies.evolutionBody: If we introduce non-essential cookies one day (for example for audience measurement), we will update this page and obtain your consent beforehand. For any questions: {email}.
